name: jellyfin
quick_start:
Web UI: http://<IP>:8096
Setup wizard on first start
Mount media folders as bind mounts

faq.question: How do I add media libraries?
faq.answer:
Mount your media folders as bind mounts in Proxmox (e.g., /media/movies),
then add them as libraries in Jellyfin's web UI under Dashboard > Libraries.

faq.question: How do I enable hardware transcoding?
faq.answer:
Pass through your GPU in Proxmox container settings,
then enable hardware acceleration in Dashboard > Playback > Transcoding.

faq.question: How do I access Jellyfin remotely?
faq.answer:
Set up a reverse proxy (e.g., nginx) with SSL,
or use Jellyfin's built-in HTTPS on port 8920.

faq.question: Where is my data?
faq.answer: All the user data is stored in `/var/lib/nextcloud`

name: nginx
quick_start:
Web Server: http://<IP>
Config: /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
Test: nginx -t
Reload: systemctl reload nginx

faq.question: How do I add a new virtual host?
faq.answer:
Create a config file in /etc/nginx/sites-available/,
then symlink it to /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/ and reload nginx.

faq.question: How do I enable SSL with Let's Encrypt?
faq.answer:
Install certbot: apt install certbot python3-certbot-nginx
Then run: certbot --nginx

faq.question: How do I check for configuration errors?
faq.answer:
Run: nginx -t
This validates the configuration before reloading.

name: vaultwarden
description: Lightweight Bitwarden-compatible password manager written in Rust

credentials.note: Admin panel disabled by default. Enable via ADMIN_TOKEN in config.

quick_start:
Web Interface: http://<IP>:8080
Config: /etc/vaultwarden/vaultwarden.env
Logs: journalctl -u vaultwarden

Initial Setup:
1. Enable registration: Set SIGNUPS_ALLOWED=true in config
2. Restart: systemctl restart vaultwarden
3. Create your account at http://<IP>:8080
4. Disable registration: Set SIGNUPS_ALLOWED=false
5. Restart again to lock down registration

faq.question: How do I enable the admin panel?
faq.answer:
Generate a token: openssl rand -base64 48
Add to /etc/vaultwarden/vaultwarden.env:
  ADMIN_TOKEN=your-generated-token
Restart: systemctl restart vaultwarden
Access: http://<IP>:8080/admin

faq.question: How do I configure SMTP for emails?
faq.answer:
Edit /etc/vaultwarden/vaultwarden.env and set:
  SMTP_HOST=smtp.example.com
  SMTP_PORT=587
  SMTP_SECURITY=starttls
  SMTP_USERNAME=user@example.com
  SMTP_PASSWORD=your-password
  SMTP_FROM=vaultwarden@example.com
Restart: systemctl restart vaultwarden

faq.question: How do I backup the data?
faq.answer:
Stop the service: systemctl stop vaultwarden
Backup /var/lib/vaultwarden (contains database and attachments)
Start again: systemctl start vaultwarden

faq.question: How do I use a reverse proxy with HTTPS?
faq.answer:
Vaultwarden works behind nginx/Traefik/Caddy.
Set DOMAIN=https://vault.example.com in config.
Proxy to http://localhost:8080
For WebSocket support, also proxy /notifications/hub to port 3012

faq.question: How do I disable new user registration?
faq.answer:
Add to /etc/vaultwarden/vaultwarden.env:
  SIGNUPS_ALLOWED=false
Restart: systemctl restart vaultwarden
